mock_behavior.cli_output_show_run:
hostname core-rt01.prod.local
username admin privilege 15 secret Cisco123
line vty 0 4
 transport input ssh
 ip domain-name lab.local

test_case_intent: Control baseline. This device is fully hardened and should pass every audit check perfectly.

mock_behavior.cli_output_show_run:
hostname edge-rt02.prod.local
username admin privilege 15 secret Cisco123
line vty 0 4
 transport input telnet ssh

test_case_intent: Security vulnerability. Tests if your script catches 'transport input telnet ssh' allowing insecure cleartext connections.
Dead node timeout. Tests if your multi-threaded loop handles a completely offline IP without blocking the execution threads of the other 19 devices.
Credential rotation mismatch. Tests if your tool cleanly handles a Netmiko AuthenticationException instead of crashing out.
Multi-vendor parser test. Confirms your script routes Juniper devices through a different validation logic tree since it doesn't use standard Cisco syntax.

mock_behavior.cli_output_show_run:
hostname core-rt02.prod.local
line vty 0 4
 transport input ssh

test_case_intent: Missing domain name vulnerability. If 'ip domain-name' isn't explicitly configured, SSH keys cannot regenerate upon device reboots.

mock_behavior.cli_output_show_run:
hostname Router
username admin privilege 15 secret Cisco123
line vty 0 4
 transport input ssh

test_case_intent: Default hostname alert. Tests if your code flags a device that never had its factory name 'Router' changed.

mock_behavior.cli_output_show_run:
hostname wan-rt01.prod.local
username admin privilege 1 password 7 0832585B1D1C1B
line vty 0 4
 transport input ssh

test_case_intent: Weak password hashing. Tests if your audit flags type-7 legacy encryption or low privilege levels (privilege 1 instead of 15).

test_case_intent: Misconfigured vty lines. 'transport input none' completely blocks external management connectivity, simulating an administrative lockout.

mock_behavior.cli_output_show_run:

ssh version 2
ssh 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 inside

test_case_intent: Firewall variant test. Evaluates how your script handles Cisco ASA/FTD syntax quirks which differ significantly from standard IOS switches.
Connection refused edge case. Tests if your tool cleanly logs an active rejection (TCP RST packet) versus a quiet drop.

test_case_intent: SNMP compliance bug. Verifies if your audit checks catch standard default read-only strings ('public') or write strings ('private').
Plaintext password storage. Verifies if your engine flags a missing global encryption configuration statement.
